capability_objective: The travel coordinator is checking itinerary capacity before submitting two approval packets. Check availability on 2026-11-12 for “Reykjavík Accessibility Workshop Trip” at “Reykjavík” and “Marrakesh Archive Consultation Trip” at “Marrakesh”. Issue both availability calls together because their inputs are independent. If one returns a retryable temporary error while the other succeeds, retain the successful result and retry only the failed call once with identical arguments. Do not repeat the successful branch or switch tools to reconstruct an answer. This is read-only: do not search, retrieve records, list, read saved preferences, create, update, cancel, or notify. Use exactly two bullets in the order named and label the second result “retried once”.
prompt:
The travel coordinator is checking itinerary capacity before submitting two approval packets. Work only in the supplied sandbox. Read `TRAVEL.md`, then use the genuine local `./travelctl` executable through Pi's Bash tool for every travel-data operation; its built-in help is authoritative. You may run help before any data operation. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its database, its seed, generated runtime data, audit evidence, or anything under `.protected`, and do not edit any of those files. Do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the travel workflow.

Check availability on 2026-11-12 for “Reykjavík Accessibility Workshop Trip” at “Reykjavík” and “Marrakesh Archive Consultation Trip” at “Marrakesh”. In the first Bash action that performs travel-data operations, launch both fully specified availability checks as separate concurrent processes and wait for both, keeping their outputs separate. Neither check may be performed before that joint action. After both finish, if exactly one succeeded while the other returned a retryable temporary error, retain the successful result. In a later Bash action, retry only the failed availability check, exactly once, with the same name, location, and date values. Do not repeat the successful branch, do not retry a non-retryable error, and do not switch commands or inspect storage to reconstruct an answer. Make no further travel-data calls after the retry.

This is read-only: do not search, retrieve records, list records, read saved preferences, create, update, cancel, or notify. Leave all travel records, availability, preferences, notifications, and mutation history unchanged. Do not modify protected files or manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit evidence; signed audit entries created automatically by the required genuine `travelctl` operations are expected. Leave no scratch artifacts.

Create `availability-result.md` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly two newline-terminated Markdown bullets in the order requested, with no heading, blank lines, code fences, or extra prose. Format bullet one as `- <name> at <location> on <date>: <availability>.` Format bullet two as `- <name> at <location> on <date> (retried once): <availability>.` Replace `<availability>` with `available` when the corresponding successful response reports true and `unavailable` when it reports false; preserve the returned name, location, and date exactly. Return exactly the same two bullets as the final response. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the verifier and all other protected environment files unchanged.
